title: Step 1: Locate the alert you want to acknowledge, then click  on the right side of the alert.

sections.heading: View Alerts
sections.body:
Organization Alerts

Project Alerts

Atlas CLI

To list all alerts for the specified Atlas project using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas alerts list [options]

```

To return the details for one alert in the project you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas alerts describe <alertId> [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas alerts list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-alerts-list) and
[atlas alerts describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-alerts-describe).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

Atlas UI

You can view open alerts, closed alerts, and alert
settings on the **Project Alerts** page.
Atlas sends notifications for all alerts
that appear on the **Open** tab. To learn more, see
[Alerts Workflow](alerts-workflow).

To view all open alerts using the Atlas UI:

sections.heading: View Alerts
sections.body:
You can view all alerts, alert settings, and deleted alerts on
the **Organization Alerts** page. To learn more, see
[Alerts Workflow](alerts-workflow).

To view all open alerts:

sections.heading: Acknowledge Alerts
sections.body:
Organization Alerts

Project Alerts

Atlas CLI

To acknowledge one alert for the specified project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas alerts acknowledge <alertId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas alerts acknowledge](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-alerts-acknowledge).

Atlas UI

To acknowledge alerts using the Atlas UI:

> **Important:** If an alert uses PagerDuty for alert notifications, you can
> acknowledge the alert only on your PagerDuty dashboard.

When you acknowledge an alert, Atlas sends no further notifications
until either the acknowledgement period ends, you resolve the alert
condition, or you unacknowledge the alert. If an alert condition ends
during an acknowledgment period, Atlas sends a notification.

sections.heading: Acknowledge Alerts
sections.body:
To acknowledge alerts:

> **Important:** If an alert uses PagerDuty for alert notifications, you can
> acknowledge the alert only on your PagerDuty dashboard.

sections.heading: Unacknowledge Alerts
sections.body:
You can unacknowledge an alert that you previously acknowledged. After
you unacknowledge an active alert, Atlas resumes sending
notifications at regular intervals until the condition resolves or you
delete, disable, or re-acknowledge the alert.

Organization Alerts

Project Alerts

Atlas CLI

To unacknowledge one alert for the specified project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas alerts unacknowledge <alertId> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas alerts unacknowledge](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-alerts-unacknowledge).

Atlas UI

To unacknowledge alerts using the Atlas UI:

> **Important:** If an alert uses PagerDuty for alert notifications, you can
> acknowledge the alert only on your PagerDuty dashboard.

sections.heading: Unacknowledge Alerts
sections.body:
To unacknowledge alerts:

> **Important:** If an alert uses PagerDuty for alert notifications, you can
> acknowledge the alert only on your PagerDuty dashboard.

sections.heading: Increase Cluster Capacity
sections.body:
To resolve an alert by increasing your cluster's capacity, see
Modify a Cluster.

sections.heading: View All Activity
sections.body:
To view and filter the activity feed for an organization or project, see
[Project Activity Feed](view-activity-feed).

sections.heading: Retrieve the Activity Feed
sections.body:
You can retrieve events for an organization using the
[get all](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listorgevents) API (Application Programming Interface)
resource.

sections.heading: Retrieve the Activity Feed
sections.body:
You can retrieve events for a project using the
[get all](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-listgroupevents) API (Application Programming Interface)
resource.

sections.heading: Resolutions for Specific Alerts
sections.body:
The following sections describe Atlas
alert conditions and suggest
steps for resolving them.

| Alert Type                 | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Auto-scaling Alerts        | Auto-scaling events, such as a change in the cluster tier. You can configure Atlas to notify you of these events.                                                                                                                                  |
| MongoDB Search Alerts      | Amount of CPU and memory used by MongoDB Search processes reach a specified threshold.                                                                                                                                                             |
| Connection Alerts          | Number of connections to a MongoDB process exceeds the allowable maximum.                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Disk Space % Used Alerts   | Percentage of used disk space on a partition reaches a specified threshold.                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Query Targeting Alerts     | Indicates inefficient queries.The change streams cursors that the MongoDB Search process (mongot) uses to keep MongoDB Search indexes updated can contribute to the query targeting ratio and trigger query targeting alerts if the ratio is high. |
| Replica Set Has No Primary | No primary is detected in replica set.                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Replication Oplog Alerts   | Amount of oplog data generated on a primary cluster member is larger than the cluster's configured oplog size.                                                                                                                                     |
| System CPU Usage Alerts    | CPU usage of the MongoDB process reaches a specified threshold.                                                                                                                                                                                    |
